sections.title: Ammo
body: Each turn when a weapon with 3+ inches of range makes an attack, after resolving the attack roll 1 D6. On a 2+ the weapon passes the ammo check. Otherwise place an ammunition marker next to the model to symbolize that it has run out of ammunition.

sections.title: Cover
body: When a model is obscured or has a piece of terrain blocking the sights of the attacker then that unit is considered in cover. While in cover the following benefits apply:
-1 to be hit
+1 to save dice

sections.title: Cells
body: At the start of each player turn that player gains 1 cell if at least one model has any STM. This can be altered by abilities.
The player can have up to a maximum of 5 cell points. Once 5 has been reached the player must remove 1 STM from each unit under their control, to a minimum of 0.
Cells can also be obtained by destroying enemy models. Each enemy model destroyed is 1 cell point gained.
If you have 0-1 cells remaining all your units have -1 to their hit rolls.

sections.title: Strains
body: The following are universal strains for units to use. Each strain specifies how many cells are needed to use it.
Reroll - 1 cell
Cover fire - 2 cells
Chill - 1 cell
Burn - 1 cell
Shuffle - 2 cells
Reinforcements - 5 cells

sections.title: Reroll
body: At the start of your turn you may use this strain.
Roll 1 D3. That number is the number of dice you may reroll this turn.

sections.title: Cover fire
body: When your opponent moves an enemy within 12 inches of a friendly model, that friendly model may make a normal move.

After that model has made a normal move you may select another model that is also within 12 inches and has line of sight of the enemy model. That model may shoot at the enemy model if they are an eligible target.

sections.title: Chill
body: When a friendly unit would be destroyed you may use this strain, excluding vehicles.
All their remaining STM is lost and their remaining wounds are changed to 1.

sections.title: Burn
body: When a friendly unit would be destroyed you may use this strain, excluding vehicles.
Before the model is removed it may make 2 STM actions, not of the same action, before dying.

sections.title: Shuffle
body: Move a model 1".

sections.title: Reinforcements
body: Select the equivalent of up to 6 wounds of dead models and put them back on the table within 2" of each other and 2" of your side of the board.
You cannot add leaders again.

sections.title: Actions
body: Normal move - 1 STM
Attack - 1 STM
Charge - 1 STM
Mission action - 1 STM
Close combat - 1 STM

sections.title: Actions
body: Normal move: that model may move up to their move characteristic.
Attack: select a weapon that the model is equipped with and has 3+ range. That model may make an attack with that weapon.

Charge: select an enemy model within 6in of that model. Roll 1 D6. If the result is equal to or greater than the distance between the 2 models the charge is successful and does not require STM. If it fails they still move but lose 1 STM.
Mission action: the mission action is specified on the mission rules.
Close combat: select a weapon with less than 3in of range and make an attack with that weapon.

sections.title: Weapons
body: RNG: how close the target must be. 3+ inches is a ranged weapon. 1 or 2 inches is a melee weapon.
ATK: how many dice you roll for the attack.
HIT: the result needed on the dice for a hit.
STR: for each 2 STR more than the toughness the opponent rolls 1 less SD. For each 2 STR less than the toughness the opponent rolls 1 more SD.

AOE: after deciding the fate of the original target roll for all models, friendly and enemy, from the original target point of view. Melee weapons are different: roll for everyone within AOE range of the initial target from the attacker perspective.
DMG: how much damage each successful wound does.
